title: Optimized Bubble Sort with Incorrect Termination Condition

statement.markdown: Implement an optimized bubble sort function in JavaScript. The function should take an array of integers as input and return the sorted array. However, the implementation has a logical bug in the termination condition, causing the function to terminate prematurely.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be an array of integers, e.g. [5, 2, 8, 1, 9].
statement.outputFormat: The sorted array of integers, e.g. [1, 2, 5, 8, 9].
statement.notes: The optimized bubble sort implementation should use a flag to track whether any swaps were made in a pass.

examples.explanation: The function should sort the input array in ascending order.
The function should return the already sorted input array.

hints.text: Review the termination condition in the bubble sort implementation.

editorial.title: Optimized Bubble Sort Implementation
editorial.intuition: The optimized bubble sort implementation uses a flag to track whether any swaps were made in a pass. This allows the function to terminate early if the input array is already sorted.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would involve using a nested loop to compare each pair of adjacent elements and swap them if they are in the wrong order.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would involve using a flag to track whether any swaps were made in a pass.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution would involve using a flag to track whether any swaps were made in a pass and terminating the function early if the input array is already sorted.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the optimized bubble sort implementation can be proved by showing that it terminates when the input array is already sorted and produces the correct sorted array when the input array is not sorted.
editorial.dryRun: Perform a dry run on the input array [5, 2, 8, 1, 9] to see how the optimized bubble sort implementation works.
commonMistakes: Failing to terminate the function early when the input array is already sorted
Not using a flag to track whether any swaps were made in a pass
